mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting corporate valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending expectations and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand growth, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production below -1.0% reflects immediate supply-side contraction, forcing firms to cut output and earnings guidance, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of triggered observations, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation accelerating above 3.0% year-over-year forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are non-negative in > 60% of high-inflation triggers, or predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is held constant.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate indicates labor market inefficiency and hidden weakness, signaling impending economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the month.
failure_condition: Interaction signal fails to predict negative returns with > 55% accuracy, or joint effect is statistically indistinguishable from independent effects.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP exceeding 2.0% confirms strong macroeconomic momentum, supporting higher corporate earnings forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of high-GDP-growth quarters, or rolling Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expanding while inflation remains subdued below 2.5% year-over-year creates a favorable supply-demand balance, allowing firms to increase margins without triggering rate hikes, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns turn negative in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal loses predictive significance when controlling for FEDFUNDS.
